items.title: Idaho hemp growers slash acreage for 2026 as demand fails to catch up with supply
items.summary: After quickly becoming one of the nation’s leading hemp fiber-producing states a few years ago, Idaho growers say they are planting just 233 acres in 2026, an 81% drop from […]
items.content_preview: After quickly becoming one of the nation’s leading hemp fiber-producing states a few years ago, Idaho growers say they are planting just 233 acres in 2026, an 81% drop from last year and the state’s lowest level since hemp production was legalized in the state. The sharply lower acreage, reported by
